hero.headline: Smart Contract Escrow made easy
hero.subhead: Using blockchain to reduce your transaction costs by 95%

features.headline:
Supercharge Your Payment
with Refundable Escrow

items.title:
Refundable
escrow
items.description: Shake uses a smart-contract escrow — like a neutral digital safe — that locks funds according to transparent rules on the blockchain. Shake never holds your money, and no one can move funds outside the agreed terms.

items.title: Pay with any crypto you own
items.description: Merchants set their preferred currency, but you can pay with what you have. Use our built-in swap feature to convert Bitcoin, Tether (USDT), or Staked ETH to match what the merchant needs — seamlessly and securely.

items.title: Trust transactions – and each other
items.description: Every transaction is sealed with a signature, and every person has a reputation – so you can be confident that the person you're talking to is trustworthy.

items.title: Convenience that Empowers
items.description: Add product info right into your contract with AI tools. Each transaction's details provide a more complete picture of the exchange, and you can add as many images as you want.

howToUse.headline: Getting Started is Easy
howToUse.subhead: Follow these simple steps to get started with Shake.

steps.title: Create an account
steps.description: Sign up for a free account in a few minutes with an ID, phone number and a few selfies.

steps.title: Add a payment method
steps.description: Fund your payments using crypto you already own, or use Coinbase Onramp to purchase crypto directly. Need a different currency? Our integrated swap feature lets you convert between supported cryptocurrencies.

steps.title: Start transacting
steps.description: Begin sending and receiving with complete confidence because Shake is non-custodial. When you're ready to cash out, Coinbase Offramp lets you convert crypto back to fiat. Shake never touches your funds during this process.

business.label: Merchant
business.headline:
Get paid
automatically
business.description: Set your escrow period, share it with your buyer, and receive funds at the end of the window — automatically. No bank, no manual release, no waiting. Set the period to 0 for an instant transfer — you'll still get identity verification, a signed agreement, and buyer ratings.

customer.label: Buyer
customer.headline:
Pay with
a safety net
customer.description: Deposit funds into escrow through the app. Your money is held securely for the agreed period. If something goes wrong, you can request a refund before the window closes.

hero.headline: How Shake's Payment System Keeps You Safe, Literally

section1.headline: The Escrow Process
section1.description:
The Shake escrow is powered by a smart contract on the blockchain. Once a payment is made, funds are locked by code — not by Shake, not by a bank, and not by a third party. 

Shake never takes custody of user funds. Payments are held by the smart contract itself, while wallet access and currency exchange are provided by Coinbase's embedded wallet and Onramp/Offramp services. This design removes single-point-of-failure risk and ensures funds can only move according to the agreed rules.

cta.subhead: Create your account today and join the movement to everyday crypto.

howItWorks.escrowIntro: Shake uses an escrow which is like a safe that keeps the customer's money secure while a business delivers a product or service.
howItWorks.agreementNote: Any escrow should have an agreement that the buyer and seller both sign before money is exchanged. AI in the app suggests an agreement, and pictures you take are included. Just sign with your finger.
howItWorks.smartContractNote: Under the hood, it's a smart contract: the release timer is written to the blockchain at the moment of payment and cannot be changed by anyone, including Shake. Funds have exactly two exits — to the merchant once the timer expires, or back to the buyer if a refund is issued. No other path exists in the code.

process.step1: After making a transaction, your money is placed into an escrow for a set amount of time, which is like a neutral safe, rather than going directly to the merchant.
process.step2: During this waiting period, if you don't receive what you purchased, you can discuss it with the merchant directly or request a refund.
process.step3: If you don't request a refund during the waiting period, the merchant can withdraw the money.

hero.headline: Grow profits with a rock solid handshake
hero.description: Shake is a secure way to receive crypto payments for your goods and services, whether you have employees or work solo.

sections.headline:
Keep More of
What You Earn
sections.description: Credit card processors typically charge 2.5–3% per transaction. Shake charges 0.1% — that's 95% lower. No terminals to buy, no bank in the middle, no international surcharges. Just a simple fee tied directly to the transaction, so more of every sale stays with you.

sections.headline:
Adapts
to You
sections.description: Whether you have employees or are flying solo, Shake's dependable payment process and lack of rigid income requirements make it easier to focus on your work. Fully mobile payments mean no terminals to buy or maintain. Withdrawals are secure even for international sales.

sections.headline:
Accept Crypto
from Anyone
sections.description: Set your preferred payment currency — USDC or ETH — and let customers pay with what they have. Through integrated currency swapping, buyers can easily convert Bitcoin, Tether (USDT), or Staked ETH into your preferred currency. The swap interface guides them through the conversion process in just a few clicks. Swap feature is powered by Odos.

sections.headline:
Convenience
that Empowers
sections.description: Add product info right into your contract with AI tools. Each transaction's details provide a more complete picture of the exchange, and you can add as many images as you want. Assign managers to oversee payments and employees to handle sales.

sections.headline:
More Options for
What You Do
sections.description: Shake is accessible to local shops, freelancers and even those without a bank account – helping more people build their business. Accept payments in dollars or crypto. Send and receive large payments without the usual red tape.

cta.header: Ready to get paid with Shake?
cta.subhead: Create your account today and experience the future of crypto payments

hero.headline:
A Payment app
built for real life
hero.description:
Shake is the new way to pay that
takes your spending seriously, like you do.

sections.headline:
Transactions
That Matter
sections.description: Pay with an app that keeps payments private, documented, and easy to resolve. Your payment is locked in a smart-contract escrow for a fixed period agreed to by both buyer and merchant. During this time, funds cannot be accessed outside the contract's rules, and disputes can be raised if the agreed terms are not met. Shake and Persona verify every business's identity, reputation, and feedback. Each transaction comes with a signed agreement and verifiable on-chain proof of payment – so you can trust your purchases and avoid disputes.

sections.headline: Level Up as a Sensible Spender
sections.description: Shake shows you the true cost of every transaction, helps you build a real reputation, and keeps your spending in check by using only what you have. It's the perfect compliment to a conscious spender's arsenal. In the long run, you avoid the baggage that comes with borrowing debt, interest, and overspending.

sections.headline: Pay with the crypto you already own
sections.description: Don't let payment limitations hold you back. If a merchant requests USDC or ETH, you can pay using Bitcoin, Tether (USDT), or Staked ETH. Simply transfer your crypto to your Coinbase wallet in the app, then use the built-in swap feature powered by Odos to convert it instantly to the required currency.

sections.headline:
Accountable
Where it Counts
sections.description: Every person on Shake is accountable for their actions, whether it's a business or an individual. Your identity isn't publicly tied to your on-chain activity. Only the merchant you're paying sees the information needed to fulfill the purchase, and reputation details are visible only to the counterparty for refunds and dispute resolution.

sections.headline:
Power Back
to the People
sections.description: Shake is accessible to local shops, freelancers and even those without a bank account – so your payments support the people you choose, not big financial institutions. If you want a refund, Shake acts as a fair and neutral middleman that makes evidence-backed decisions.

cta.header: Ready to start paying with Shake?
cta.subhead: Create your account today and experience the future of crypto payments

faq.headline: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subhead: Find answers to common questions about the Shake app.

noResults.title: No results found for

questions.question: How does the app work?
questions.answer: Shake uses an escrow, which is like a safe, that keeps the customer's money secure while a business delivers a product or service. To learn more, see the "How it Works" page.

questions.question: How do I get started?
questions.answer: You can get started by [downloading the app on your mobile device](https://shakedefi.com).

questions.question: What is an escrow?
questions.answer:
An escrow is like a safe that keeps the money secure and ensures both the customer and business are fulfilling their obligations before the exchange is made. 

For example, let's say you're buying a used phone from someone you found online. Normally, you'd meet up, pay them on the spot, and hope the phone works. But if it's broken or not what they promised, it's already too late — the money's gone. 

With escrow, instead of paying them directly, your money goes into a secure holding account for a fixed amount of time. The seller sees the money is there, and hands over the phone. 

During the waiting period, you check the quality of the phone. If you discover it's broken, you would dispute the transaction and request a refund. If the phone works, the money is released to the seller once the waiting period is over. 

In essence, the escrow:    
1) Protects businesses by ensuring that the customer's payment is ready and available for transfer once the waiting period is over    
2) Provides protection for both parties by ensuring funds are held securely and can only be released according to the agreed escrow terms, including refunds when warranted by a dispute outcome. 

To learn more, see the "How it Works" page.

questions.question: Do I have to use an escrow period?
questions.answer: No. Merchants set the escrow period, and it can be set to 0 for an instant transfer with no waiting and no refund window. You still get all of Shake's other protections: identity verification, a signed agreement with photos, thorough transaction documentation, and buyer ratings. The escrow period is the right choice when you want to give buyers a safety net — instant transfer is the right choice when the sale is straightforward and both parties are ready.

questions.question: Do I need to know about crypto / invest in crypto to use Shake?
questions.answer: No, you do not need to know about or invest in crypto to use Shake. That's the magic of it.

questions.question: Who can use the Shake app?
questions.answer: Shake is available for those over the age of 18. Creating a Shake account requires identity verification from the Persona service (withpersona.com).

questions.question: Can I pay with any cryptocurrency I own?
questions.answer:
Yes! Shake supports flexible payment options through integrated currency swapping. If a merchant requests USDC or ETH, you can pay using Bitcoin, Tether (USDT), or Staked ETH. Simply transfer your cryptocurrency to your Coinbase wallet in the app, then use the built-in Odos swap feature to convert it to the required currency. This means merchants can now accept crypto from anyone, regardless of which cryptocurrency they hold.

Note: Coinbase and Odos swap services are not available in all countries.

questions.question: Do I have to accept any 'arbitrary' cryptocurrency as payment?
questions.answer: No, you specify which crypto you want to receive (USDC or ETH). When buyers pay with a different cryptocurrency like Bitcoin or Dogecoin, they use the built-in swap feature to convert it to your preferred currency before the transaction completes. You always receive exactly what you requested. When it comes time to withdraw your funds, we integrate with Coinbase to withdraw them to any account Coinbase can access.

questions.question: How long does it take to send or accept a payment? Is there an instant transfer fee?
questions.answer: Payments typically confirm on-chain in seconds. The "waiting" you see in Shake isn't transfer time—it's escrow protection. You choose an escrow period (for example, 3 days). After that, there's a standard 2-day arbitration buffer in case a dispute is filed; if there's no dispute, funds release automatically when that buffer ends. If a merchant wants to get paid during the 2-day buffer, they can use early withdrawal for an additional fee (up to 1%): withdrawing "2 days early" means getting paid right when your chosen escrow period ends. Shake does not charge a separate instant transfer fee; normal network fees may apply.

questions.question: Are there fees to use Shake?
questions.answer:
Shake charges a simple, transparent service fee for escrowed transactions. The standard fee is 0.1% of the transaction amount. 

If a merchant chooses to withdraw funds early (before the escrow period ends), an additional early withdrawal fee applies, up to 1% for a 2-day early withdrawal. 

Blockchain network fees may also apply, and are paid directly to the network. Currency conversion (fiat ↔ crypto) is handled by Coinbase Onramp and Offramp, which may apply their own fees depending on the asset used. Currency swapping between cryptocurrencies uses the Odos protocol and includes standard swap fees.

Shake does not add hidden fees, does not take custody of funds, and does not charge recurring subscription fees for basic use.

questions.question: Do I need to report my Shake transactions on my taxes?
questions.answer:
In most countries, including the United States, cryptocurrency transactions are taxable events. This applies to Shake transactions as well. There are three main transaction types to be aware of:

1) Paying for goods or services with crypto is treated as a disposal of that asset. If the crypto's value increased since you acquired it, you may owe capital gains tax on the difference.
2) Swapping between cryptocurrencies (e.g., converting Bitcoin to USDC via Shake's built-in swap feature) is also generally treated as a taxable event in the U.S.
3) Receiving crypto as a merchant is typically treated as ordinary income at the fair market value at the time of receipt.

Crypto tax tools like [CoinLedger](https://coinledger.io) and [Koinly](https://koinly.io) make this process straightforward. They can import your on-chain transaction history — including Shake transactions, which are permanently recorded on the blockchain — and automatically calculate your gains, losses, and income.

questions.question: Are there any income requirements to use Shake?
questions.answer: No, there are no income requirements to use Shake.

questions.question: Can I accept international transactions with Shake?
questions.answer: Yes, you can accept international transactions from any other Shake user.

questions.question: In which countries is Shake not available?
questions.answer: Shake is not available in Brazil, Mexico, Taiwan, Japan, and United Kingdom.

questions.question: Are there any spending requirements, limits or transaction caps?
questions.answer: There are no spending limits or transaction caps imposed by Shake itself.

questions.question: What happens if there is a dispute?
questions.answer: Shake's process records metadata and provides transaction information to resolve disputes. Shake is the default arbiter for disputes. Arbiters issue refunds when warranted. Our next feature is to allow the buyer and seller to delegate any Shake user as the arbiter for their transaction.

questions.question: How does Shake protect against fraud and unauthorized transactions?
questions.answer: Shake protects against fraud and unauthorized transactions with verified identities, signed agreements for each transaction, and verifiable on-chain proof of payment, backed by detailed records for dispute resolution.

questions.question: Why can't Shake or a hacker take my money and run?
questions.answer:
Shake is non-custodial and does not have custody of user funds. Payments are held by a smart contract that can only release funds to the merchant after the escrow period, or back to the buyer in the case of a refund. 

Shake does not store your wallet private keys — Coinbase does. This means Shake cannot access, move, or redirect escrowed funds. Coinbase provides wallet infrastructure and currency exchange, but escrow logic is enforced by blockchain code — not by human discretion.

questions.question: What is documented when I make a transaction?
questions.answer: Shake encrypts all data and logs every detail: Time, location, signatures, and photos that you provide. Each party's device also takes a front-facing photo of the signer as they are signing. Delivery confirmation can be uploaded, and the system confirms the money is available before a transaction happens.

questions.question: How does Shake create accountability?
questions.answer: Every Shake user has a verified identity and feedback score. Persona checks each member's ID, so no one can make unlimited wallets to hide from accountability.

questions.question: What information is public?
questions.answer: While transactions are recorded on the blockchain (which is publicly viewable but anonymous), personal Shake accounts and blockchain accounts are not publicly associated.

questions.question: Is there support for splitting shared expenses amongst a group?
questions.answer: While this isn't currently supported, it is on our roadmap for future implementation.

questions.question: Where is Shake Defi incorporated?
questions.answer: Shake Defi, Inc. is incorporated in Brookfield, Wisconsin, United States .

cta.subhead: The Shake team is here to help with any questions you may have.
